title: Nefta

{{Quote|Palpatine should have known…we can't wait indefinitely. The ''throne'' cannot sit empty or the Empire will ''collapse!''<nowiki></nowiki>|Nefta|<ref name="DE II 1" />}}
'''Nefta''', a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was a member of the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Circle]] and a [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] loyal to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during his reign in [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] bodies. After Palpatine was [[Battle of Pinnacle Base|killed]] by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], Nefta—along with another [[darksider]], [[Sa-Di]]—decided to take his place instead of waiting until he [[Transfer essence|returned]] in a new clone body. The adepts destroyed the Emperor's clones that had survived an [[Duel on Byss|attack]] attempted previously by Skywalker. Palpatine's loyal [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Executor]] [[Sedriss QL|Sedriss]], however, witnessed Nefta's and Sa-Di's treachery and [[Death/Legends|killed]] them for it.

==Biography==
{{Quote|One ''less'' future Emperor—one death ''closer'' to power over the ''galaxy!''<nowiki></nowiki>|Nefta, while destroying the clone tanks|<ref name="DE II 1" />}}
Nefta was a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="DE II 1" /> member of the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Circle]]<ref name="Pawns" /> and a [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] serving [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]],<ref name="DE II 1" /> who had been [[Transfer essence|reborn]] in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body<ref name="Dark Empire 2">[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]]</ref> after his [[Death/Legends|death]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi">{{Film|VI}}</ref> In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Palpatine launched an [[Operation Shadow Hand|assault]] on the Alliance—which had taken over dominant governmental and territorial control of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]—but was [[Battle of Pinnacle Base|killed]] by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]],<ref name="Dark Empire 6">[[Dark Empire 6|''Dark Empire'' 6]]</ref> who also [[Duel on Byss|destroyed]] Palpatine's clones stored in the [[Emperor's Citadel]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]].<ref name="Dark Empire 5">[[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]]</ref> Skywalker, however, was not aware that Palpatine had an additional hidden storage of clones in that same citadel. Following the Emperor's death, [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Executor]] [[Sedriss QL|Sedriss]]—a member of the [[Dark Side Elite]], the select cadre of Palpatine's [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]—assumed command of the Imperial forces, hoping that one day Palpatine's spirit would inhabit another clone and the Emperor would return to rule the Empire once more.<ref name="DE II 1" />

[[File:SedrisskillsNefta.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Sedriss QL|Sedriss]] kills Nefta.]]
Nefta, however, thought that the Emperor's return could occur many years later, and without a leader, the Empire could collapse beforehand. Nefta and fellow [[darksider]] [[Sa-Di]] wanted to take the throne themselves and decided to ensure that Palpatine would not come back to stand in their way. The two first spread rumors of the Emperor's permanent demise, and later, when Sedriss departed to [[Battle of Balmorra|discipline]] the recently rebelled planet of [[Balmorra/Legends|Balmorra]], made their final move. Nefta and Sa-Di, armed with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and an [[Axe/Legends|axe]] respectively, assaulted the secret storage of clones and destroyed all cloning tanks one by one. Just as they finished their mission, Sedriss returned from Balmorra and witnessed the results of their work. The Executor immediately decided to kill the two for their treachery and [[Skirmish on Byss|attacked]] Nefta with his lightsaber. Nefta blocked the first blow with his own weapon but was then quickly slain by Sedriss, who later executed Sa-Di as well. Unbeknownst to all three, Palpatine had in fact already taken a new clone body and was watching the entire scene from the shadows in a corridor. After Nefta's and Sa-Di's deaths, the Emperor revealed himself and acknowledged Sedriss's loyalty toward him.<ref name="DE II 1" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|One powerful ''individual'' must rule—but certainly that won't be ''you!''<nowiki></nowiki>|Sedriss to Nefta|<ref name="DE II 1" />}}
Nefta was an ambitious individual who was willing to rule the Galactic Empire. Although considered to be one of Palpatine's closest friends, Nefta betrayed him by destroying the clones, a task he found most distasteful, but necessary to preserve the Empire.<ref name="DE II 1" />

==Powers and abilities==
[[File:SedrissVersusNefta.jpg|thumb|right|225px|Nefta engages Sedriss in combat.]]
{{Quote|What's the matter, Nefta—is your ''magic'' of no use to you against a ''dark-side warrior?''<nowiki></nowiki>|Sedriss|<ref name="DE II 1" />}}
Nefta was able to adequately handle a lightsaber, using one to destroy the clone tanks. He also apparently possessed some [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]], which Sedriss referred to as "magic," although neither Nefta's skill with the lightsaber nor his powers were enough to defeat the Executor.<ref name="DE II 1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Nefta's only appearance in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] is in the [[Dark Empire II 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]]. Nefta and Sa-Di are the only two characters in the entire comic series whose lines in the speech bubbles are written in white on the black background, instead of the traditional black on white.<ref name="DE II 1" /> Nefta is completely absent from the [[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|audio adaptation]] of ''Dark Empire II'', in which Sa-Di destroys the clones by himself.<ref name="DE II audio">[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]]</ref>

His name is most likely a reference to [[Wikipedia:Nafta, Tunisia|Nefta]], a village in [[Tunisia]] where scenes from the [[1977]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] were filmed.<ref name="Movie Locations">{{WebCite|url=http://www.movie-locations.com/movies/s/starwars.html|text=''Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope'' film locations|archivedate=20100103114840|nolive=1}}</ref>

In addition to his sole appearance, Nefta was briefly referenced in several sources, including the [[2002]] ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''<ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> and ''[[The Emperor's Pawns]]'', an article appearing in the [[Star Wars Gamer 5|fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine. That article contains a continuity error, as it states that the [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] [[Sarcev Quest]] betrayed Nefta and Sa-Di when abusing the power within the Ruling Council.<ref name="Pawns" /> Quest's rise to power, however, occurred after the final [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|death]] of Palpatine<ref name="EE 2">[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]]</ref> in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], a year after Sedriss killed Nefta and Sa-Di.<ref name="NEC" />
